products.description:
A natural imperial topaz weighing 108.6 grams mined in Pakistan.

products.title: Sky Blue Topaz Emerald Cut
products.description:
A natural sky blue topaz cut as an emerald cut, from Pakistan.

Hand-selected for colour and life rather than paper grades. Sky blue, Swiss blue and London blue describe increasing depth of colour, not different species. Imperial topaz — the natural golden to peach-pink material — is far rarer than any blue topaz and priced accordingly.

**Care.** Topaz has perfect basal cleavage — a sharp knock in the wrong direction can split it regardless of its hardness. Never ultrasonic or steam. Avoid prolonged strong light for pink material.

**Treatment.** Blue topaz is almost always irradiated and heated colourless topaz. The treatment is permanent and the material is safe. Imperial topaz is naturally coloured.

products.originalTitle: Fine Ruby 0.55ct Oval - Classic Red Gemstone
products.treatment: Heat treatment is near-universal and stable. Lead-glass filling is a separate, lower-value treatment that must always be disclosed.
products.title: Ruby 0.55ct Oval Cut
products.description:
A natural ruby weighing 0.55 carats cut as an oval, from Pakistan.

Hand-selected for colour and life rather than paper grades. Pigeon blood — a vivid pure red with a faint blue undertone and strong fluorescence — is the benchmark grade. Certification from GUILD, GRS or SSEF matters most above 1 carat, where price per carat rises steeply.

**Care.** Extremely durable. Safe for ultrasonic cleaning unless the stone is glass-filled or heavily fractured — glass-filled rubies must only be cleaned with soap and water.

**Treatment.** Heat treatment is near-universal and stable. Lead-glass filling is a separate, lower-value treatment that must always be disclosed.
products.metaDescription: Ruby 0.55ct Oval Cut. Colour (pigeon blood premium), treatment status, clarity, size. Natural, ethically sourced from Pakistan. One-of-a-kind stone.

products.treatment: Minor to moderate oiling or resin filling of surface-reaching fissures is standard and accepted trade practice.
products.title: Swat Emerald 0.63ct Emerald Cut
products.description:
A natural swat emerald weighing 0.63 carats cut as an emerald cut, from Swat Valley, Pakistan.

Hand-selected for colour and life rather than paper grades. Colour saturation drives value far more than clarity. A slightly included stone with vivid, evenly distributed green outperforms a pale, clean one. Swat Valley (Pakistan) emeralds are prized for a cool, slightly bluish green and often show better transparency than Colombian material at the same price point.

**Care.** Never use ultrasonic or steam cleaners — most emeralds are oiled and heat or vibration can disturb the filler. Clean with lukewarm water, mild soap and a soft brush.

**Treatment.** Minor to moderate oiling or resin filling of surface-reaching fissures is standard and accepted trade practice.

products.description:

Hand-selected for colour and life rather than paper grades. Sapphire is corundum in every colour except red. Velvety cornflower blue carries the highest premium, but yellow and purple sapphires offer far more colour per euro and are dramatically under-priced relative to blue.

**Treatment.** Heat treatment is standard and stable. Diffusion and beryllium treatment exist and materially reduce value — always require disclosure.

products.description:
A natural yellow citrine weighing 14.03 carats cut as a radiant, from Pakistan.

Hand-selected for colour and life rather than paper grades. Honey and Madeira tones command more than pale lemon. Because large clean crystals are common, citrine is one of the best values in gemstones for statement-sized stones above 10 carats.

**Treatment.** Much commercial citrine is heat-treated amethyst. Natural citrine tends toward a paler, smokier yellow; deep orange-red material is usually treated.

products.treatment: Heat treatment and irradiation are common in pink and red material to improve colour. Usually stable.
products.title: Rubellite Tourmaline 1.75ct Oval Cut
products.description:
A natural rubellite tourmaline weighing 1.75 carats cut as an oval, from Pakistan.

Hand-selected for colour and life rather than paper grades. Tourmaline offers the widest colour range of any gem species. Indicolite (blue-teal) and rubellite (true red-pink) carry the strongest premiums. Look for even colour without a dark axis when viewed through the table.

**Care.** Warm soapy water only. Avoid ultrasonic cleaning — tourmaline is often included and can fracture. Avoid sudden temperature change.

**Treatment.** Heat treatment and irradiation are common in pink and red material to improve colour. Usually stable.
products.metaDescription: Rubellite Tourmaline 1.75ct Oval Cut. Colour rarity (indicolite and rubellite highest), clarity, size. Natural, ethically sourced from Pakistan.

products.title: Peridot 4.4ct Radiant Cut
products.description:
A natural peridot weighing 4.4 carats cut as a radiant, from Pakistan.

Hand-selected for colour and life rather than paper grades. Peridot is idiochromatic — its green comes from iron in the crystal structure itself, so every stone is green and the only question is tone. The finest is a pure grass green without brown or yellow masking, most often from Pakistan.

**Care.** Sensitive to acids and to rapid temperature change. Clean with mild soap and lukewarm water only. Never ultrasonic.

**Treatment.** Essentially never treated. Peridot reaches the market in its natural state.

products.title: Emerald 3.8ct Cabochon Cut
products.description:
A natural emerald weighing 3.8 carats cut in Pakistan.

Polished as a cabochon to show body colour and any internal character. Colour saturation drives value far more than clarity. A slightly included stone with vivid, evenly distributed green outperforms a pale, clean one. **Treatment.** Minor to moderate oiling or resin filling of surface-reaching fissures is standard and accepted trade practice.

Swat Valley (Pakistan) emeralds are prized for a cool, slightly bluish green and often show better transparency than Colombian material at the same price point.

**Care.** Never use ultrasonic or steam cleaners — most emeralds are oiled and heat or vibration can disturb the filler. Clean with lukewarm water, mild soap and a soft brush.

**Treatment.** Minor to moderate oiling or resin filling of surface-reaching fissures is standard and accepted trade practice.
